title: Oenothera filipes
taxonomy: Oenothera filipes is classified within the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. This plant is a species within the Oenothera genus, which comprises approximately 125 species of flowering plants. The classification of Oenothera filipes reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Onagraceae. Within this family, Oenothera filipes is further categorized as a part of the tribe Onagreae. The classification of Oenothera filipes is a key aspect of understanding its biology and ecology.

benefits: Oenothera filipes, also known as the fringed evening primrose, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its roots and leaves are rich in antioxidants, which may help protect against cell damage and inflammation. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could aid in reducing pain and swelling. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. constituents: Oenothera filipes is known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic compounds. These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and stems are rich in sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. parts: Oenothera filipes, also known as the thread-leaved evening primrose, is a plant species native to North America. intro: Oenothera filipes, also known as Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Onagraceae family, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible roots. The health benefits of Oenothera filipes include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions such as eczema. The bioactive constituents of this plant include gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Oenothera filipes is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wound healing, and menstrual health.
